key_insight: A single attractive crowder species can drive a full coil-globule-coil reentrant transition by saturable bridging: collapse at low crowder density and reexpansion when binding sites saturate at high density.
engineering_relevance: This identifies crowder density as a minimal control knob for tuning polymer compaction in crowded soft-matter and biomolecular environments, including charged systems.
method: Molecular dynamics simulations of coarse-grained polymers with one species of attractive crowder
Systematic variation of crowder volume fraction φc to probe collapse/reexpansion behavior
Comparison against purely repulsive crowders and charged-polymer cases with explicit counterions
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a complete reentrant transition in simulation, loss of SAW-universal size-distribution collapse under bridging, and strong crowding-driven expansion in the charged-polymer case.
watch_item: The mechanism depends on saturable binding/bridging assumptions in a coarse-grained model, so quantitative transfer to specific materials will require parameter validation.
paper_title: Density-driven reentrant polymer transitions via saturable bridging crowders
